headings.usage: Use Outfit for all primary headings, numbers, and hero text. It provides a geometric, premium tech feel.

body.usage: Use Manrope for all body text, labels, and forms. Extremely readable on small screens.

gradients.usage: Limit gradient usage to key CTAs, active tab indicators, and the borders of profile pictures/reels. DO NOT use gradients as page backgrounds.

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use backdrop-blur-xl (16px to 24px blur) with bg-white/5 and border-white/10 for sticky navs, bottom tabs, and modals.
visual_enhancers.corners: Use rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl for cards to maintain the soft, modern social app feel.
visual_enhancers.shadows: Avoid harsh drop shadows. Use ambient glows (brand gradient behind an element with blur-xl) for glowing active effects.

buttons.primary: Pill-shaped (rounded-full), bg-gradient-to-r from-violet-600 via-indigo-500 to-orange-500, text-white, font-semibold. Add hover:scale-105 transition-transform.
buttons.secondary: Glass effect: bg-white/10 border border-white/10 text-white rounded-full hover:bg-white/20.
buttons.input: Large tap targets (h-14). bg-[#121212] border-white/10 focus:border-pink-500 rounded-xl px-4 text-white.

cards.style: bg-[#121212] border border-white/10 rounded-2xl p-4 or p-6. No heavy shadows.

dev_banner.style: bg-blue-600/90 backdrop-blur-sm text-white text-sm py-2 px-4 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-between border border-blue-400/30 shadow-lg mb-4

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Mobile-first. Wrap the main app view in a mobile-proportioned container on desktop (e.g., max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen border-x border-white/10 bg-black) to emulate a phone screen.
spacing_and_layout.paddings: Use generous padding (p-6, p-8) for empty states and onboarding. Use tighter padding (p-4) for feed/dashboard items.
spacing_and_layout.navigation: Sticky bottom navigation for authenticated states (Dashboard).

animations.micro: Hover states on buttons (scale/translate), active states on tabs.
animations.loading: Use a subtle pulsing skeleton (animate-pulse bg-white/10) instead of basic spinners.

hero_vendor.description: Cinematic shot of a street vendor. Perfect for hero overlay background.

customer_role.description: Young Indian woman engaged with her phone, representing the Customer role selection.

vendor_role.description: Indian local shop owner, representing the Vendor role selection.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over gradients has sufficient contrast.
accessibility.test_ids: MUST add data-testid to all interactive and key informational elements. Examples: data-testid="login-submit-button", data-testid="otp-input".

instructions_to_main_agent: This is a standard React JS project (.js files, no .tsx syntax).
Implement a dark theme UI as the base, utilizing the defined brand gradients only as accents.
Import Outfit and Manrope fonts via Google Fonts in index.css. Apply them globally in tailwind.config.js.
Implement the landing page hero emphasizing the 'Discover local. Chat direct. Deal fair.' value proposition with an overlay background image.
For Phase 0 routing, create pages for: / (Landing), /login (OTP), /verify, /onboarding, /dashboard, /profile.
Use Shadcn UI components but heavily customize them. Remove the default Vercel look: use border-white/10, dark surfaces, and add the requested violet-indigo-orange pill buttons.
For the Dev OTP banner, it should be a distinct UI element that appears when OTP is provided directly by the auth stub API.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Do not converge toward generic 'AI slop' outputs. Use the defined gradient sparingly for high impact.
Create depth with layered elements (z-index) and glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl).
Never use `transition: all`. Target specific properties like `transition-transform` or `transition-opacity`.
Do not center-align body text. Keep forms and text content left-aligned.
Always use the provided image URLs. NEVER use placehold.it or dummyimage.com placeholders.
Use `sonner` for toast notifications (e.g., OTP sent successfully, Error logging in).
Include `data-testid` on all buttons, links, inputs, and form elements.
Avoid using complete transparent backgrounds for dropdowns or modals.
